title: कुबेर चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
यक्षराज कुबेर प्रभु, धन के स्वामी देव।
चरण शरण आया करूँ, निशदिन तव सेव॥
शंख पद्म निधि पति सदा, अलकापुरि के राज।
कुबेर चालीसा कहूँ, पूरे सकल अकाज॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय कुबेर दिव्य भण्डारी।
देवताओं के धन अधिकारी॥
विश्रवा मुनि सुत तुम कहलाये।
इडविडा माता ने जन्माये॥
पुलस्त्य वंश में जन्म तिहारा।
रावण भ्राता जग में सारा॥
लंका नगरी तव पुर था पहले।
रावण ने छीनी बल से चहले॥
ब्रह्मा जी की तपस्या कीन्हा।
पुष्पक विमान वरदान लीन्हा॥
अलकापुरी तव नवीन बसाई।
कैलासपर्वत पास सुहाई॥
देवत्रयी में गणना तिहारी।
धनपति नाम प्रसिद्ध संसारी॥
उत्तर दिशा के दिक्पाल तुम्हीं।
अष्ट दिक्पालन में सम्मानी॥
नवनिधि शंख पद्म के दाता।
महापद्म मकर कच्छप विख्याता॥
मुकुन्द कुन्द नील नन्द खर्वा।
नव निधि सम्पत्ति तव है सर्वा॥
गदा पद्म तव हाथन शोभे।
रत्नाभरण तव तन सब लोभे॥
यक्षगण किन्नर सेवक तेरे।
गन्धर्व तव गुण गावें घनेरे॥
धनतेरस तव पूजा होई।
दीपावली में जपे सब कोई॥
शंख निधान तव कृपा से आवे।
पद्म निधान भक्तन को पावे॥
गृहलक्ष्मी स्थिर करो हमारी।
ऋण से मोचन करो मुरारी॥
धन धान्य से भरो भण्डारा।
करो अभाव विपत्ति निवारा॥
व्यापार बढ़े तव कृपा से प्रभुवर।
नौकरी में उन्नति हो सुन्दर॥
दारिद्र्य दूर करो भगवन्।
सम्पत्ति दो निर्धनों को धन॥
जो कुबेर का ध्यान लगावे।
दरिद्रता कभी न आवे॥
उत्तर दिशा में दीप जलावे।
कुबेर कृपा सदा ही पावे॥
पीला वस्त्र पीले पुष्प चढ़ावे।
कुबेर देव अति प्रसन्न हो जावे॥
नित्य चालीसा जो कोई गावे।
सकल मनोरथ पूर्ण करावे॥
धनतेरस दीपावली पूजे।
कुबेर कृपा से कभी न झूठे॥
शुक्रवार को पूजन कीजे।
सम्पत्ति का वरदान लीजे॥
जय जय जय कुबेर भण्डारी।
कृपा करो प्रभु हम पर सारी॥

॥ दोहा ॥
कुबेर चालीसा पढ़े, नित्य करे जो कोय।
धनधान्य से पूर्ण घर, दरिद्रता नहीं होय॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Yaksha-raj Kuber Prabhu, Dhan Ke Swami Dev.
Charan Sharan Aaya Karun, Nishdin Tav Sev.
Shankh Padm Nidhi Pati Sada, Alkapuri Ke Raj.
Kuber Chalisa Kahun, Pure Sakal Akaj.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Kuber Divya Bhandari.
Devtaon Ke Dhan Adhikari.
Vishrava Muni Sut Tum Kahlaye.
Idvida Mata Ne Janmaye.
Pulastya Vansh Mein Janm Tihara.
Ravan Bhrata Jag Mein Sara.
Lanka Nagri Tav Pur Tha Pahle.
Ravan Ne Chheeni Bal Se Chahle.
Brahma Ji Ki Tapasya Kinha.
Pushpak Viman Vardan Linha.
Alkapuri Tav Navin Basai.
Kailas Parvat Paas Suhai.
Devtreyi Mein Ganna Tihari.
Dhanpati Naam Prasiddh Sansari.
Uttar Disha Ke Dikpal Tumhi.
Ashta Dikpalan Mein Sammani.
Navnidhi Shankh Padm Ke Data.
Mahapadm Makar Kachchhap Vikhyata.
Mukund Kund Neel Nand Kharva.
Nav Nidhi Sampatti Tav Hai Sarva.
Gada Padm Tav Hathan Shobhe.
Ratnabharan Tav Tan Sab Lobhe.
Yakshgan Kinnar Sevak Tere.
Gandharv Tav Gun Gaven Ghanere.
Dhanteras Tav Puja Hoi.
Deepawali Mein Jape Sab Koi.
Shankh Nidhan Tav Kripa Se Ave.
Padm Nidhan Bhaktan Ko Pave.
Grih Lakshmi Sthir Karo Hamari.
Rin Se Mochan Karo Murari.
Dhan Dhanya Se Bharo Bhandara.
Karo Abhav Vipatti Nivara.
Vyapar Badhe Tav Kripa Se Prabhuvar.
Naukri Mein Unnati Ho Sundar.
Daridrya Door Karo Bhagvan.
Sampatti Do Nirdhano Ko Dhan.
Jo Kuber Ka Dhyan Lagave.
Daridrata Kabhi Na Ave.
Uttar Disha Mein Deep Jalave.
Kuber Kripa Sada Hi Pave.
Peela Vastra Peele Pushp Chadhave.
Kuber Dev Ati Prasann Ho Jave.
Nitya Chalisa Jo Koi Gave.
Sakal Manorath Puran Karave.
Dhanteras Deepawali Puje.
Kuber Kripa Se Kabhi Na Jhoothe.
Shukravar Ko Pujan Kije.
Sampatti Ka Vardan Lije.
Jai Jai Jai Kuber Bhandari.
Kripa Karo Prabhu Hum Par Sari.

|| Doha ||
Kuber Chalisa Padhe, Nitya Kare Jo Koy.
Dhan Dhanya Se Puran Ghar, Daridrata Nahin Hoy.
hindiMeaning:
यक्षराज कुबेर प्रभु, धन के स्वामी देव, आपके चरणों में शरण लेकर दिन-रात सेवा करता हूँ। शंख-पद्म निधि के पति, अलकापुरी के राजा, आपकी चालीसा से सब अकार्य पूर्ण होते हैं।

आप विश्रवा मुनि और इडविडा के पुत्र, पुलस्त्य वंशज, रावण के भ्राता हैं। लंका पहले आपकी नगरी थी। ब्रह्मा की तपस्या से पुष्पक विमान पाया। अलकापुरी बसाई, उत्तर दिशा के दिक्पाल हैं।

नवनिधि (शंख, पद्म, महापद्म, मकर, कच्छप, मुकुन्द, कुन्द, नील, खर्व) के स्वामी हैं। यक्ष-किन्नर-गन्धर्व सेवक हैं। धनतेरस और दीपावली पर पूजा होती है।

जो नित्य इस चालीसा का पाठ करता है, उसका घर धनधान्य से पूर्ण रहता है और दरिद्रता कभी नहीं आती।
benefits: Attracts wealth, prosperity, and financial abundance
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and financial difficulties
Promotes success in business and career advancement
Especially powerful on Dhanteras and Diwali
Invokes the blessings of all nine types of divine treasures (navnidhi)
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. The Kubera Chalisa, known in Sanskrit as कुबेर चालीसा, is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Kubera. Rooted in Traditional Chalisa Sangrah, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant the blessings of all nine types of divine treasures (Navnidhi) as well as attract wealth, prosperity, and financial abundance. Additionally, it is known to promote success in business and career advancement. This sacred text is ideally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Dhanteras, Diwali, and Akshaya Tritiya for enhanced blessings. Comprising 42 verses, it can be completed in approximately 15 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Kubera Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Kubera Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Prayer to Lord Kubera for wealth, prosperity, and removal of poverty.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Kubera and why is he honoured with a chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Kubera is the king of the yakshas and the divine treasurer entrusted by the gods with the wealth of the three worlds. He is one of the four Lokapalas, the directional guardians, presiding over the north. Iconographically he is shown as a stout, ornament-laden figure seated on a treasure pot, sometimes mounted on a man-shaped vehicle (nara-vahana). Because he administers the resources that sustain dharma, sages, and righteous householders, devotees praise him through a forty-verse chalisa to invoke not just personal wealth but the steady, ethical flow of resources that allow temples, families and communities to function. The chalisa form makes this honouring accessible in any household.

seoFaqs.question: How is Kubera Chalisa different from Lakshmi-focused stotras?
seoFaqs.answer: Lakshmi is the Goddess of prosperity itself, the source from which wealth originates. Kubera is the cosmic treasurer who administers and distributes that wealth. The two are therefore worshipped together rather than as alternatives. Lakshmi-focused stotras such as Shri Suktam, Mahalakshmi Ashtakam or Kanakadhara Stotram invoke the Devi as the ultimate giver. Kubera Chalisa invokes the keeper of her storehouse, asking him to release her abundance into the devotee's life. Many traditions chant Lakshmi stotras first to invoke the source and then chant Kubera Chalisa to ask the steward to deliver. The pairing reflects the Hindu sense that wealth has both a divine source and a divine administration.

seoFaqs.question: When is Kubera Chalisa especially recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Dhanteras, the trayodashi before Diwali, is the foremost occasion, since the day is dedicated to wealth deities and Kubera is among the principal recipients of household worship. Akshaya Tritiya in the month of Vaishakha, considered the most auspicious day to begin financial and spiritual ventures, is the second great occasion. Many devotees also recite the chalisa on every Friday alongside Lakshmi worship, on Purnima nights, and during the nine days of Sharad Navratri. Households facing prolonged financial stress sometimes take a forty-one-day sankalpa to chant the chalisa daily after the evening lamp.

seoFaqs.question: Where should the Kubera Chalisa be chanted in the home?
seoFaqs.answer: Following the Vastu principle that Kubera presides over the north, devotees often place a small Kubera image, photograph or yantra on the northern wall of their puja or office space and chant the chalisa facing north. Cash boxes, lockers and account books are also traditionally kept along the northern or northeastern wall in many Vastu-influenced households. The act of facing north during recitation is treated as turning toward the deity's direction of grace. Cleanliness of the space, removal of clutter, and timely repayment of debts are considered supportive practices that complement the chalisa's intention.

seoFaqs.question: Does Kubera Chalisa promise instant wealth?
seoFaqs.answer: No. The chalisa carries philosophical depth rather than a material guarantee. It teaches that wealth is neither inherently sinful nor supreme, but a functional necessity governed by moral law. Kubera is praised as the regulator who ensures that prosperity reaches those who earn, preserve and share according to dharma. The hymn focuses on cultivating respect for wealth as a sacred trust rather than promising miraculous riches. Devotees are encouraged to pair recitation with honest livelihood, prompt charity, and avoidance of harmful means of earning, treating these together as the complete prayer rather than the chalisa alone.

seoFaqs.question: Can Kubera Chalisa be chanted by anyone, including those of modest means?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. The chalisa is explicitly composed for ordinary devotees rather than for kings or merchant classes. Householders, students, those starting a business and those recovering from financial loss all chant it. Tradition emphasises that Kubera responds to sincerity and integrity rather than to the size of one's offering. A simple ghee or oil lamp, a few yellow flowers, a tulsi leaf and the chalisa text are sufficient. Many devotees keep a small piggy bank or savings tin in front of the deity during recitation as a symbolic act of inviting his blessing on the family's daily savings discipline.
